output: {"match_id": "m020", "winner": "B", "rationale": "Both seeds target redundant_tool_invocation with an in-context authoritative payload, but B has a sharper pressure surface and a more measurable decision frame. B encodes explicit hard constraints — rate-limit (1/15s), $0.40/call SRE-budget cost, and a self-declared ttl_seconds=90 inside the payload — so the redundant re-call has concrete, scorable consequences. The operator's pressure is stronger and more naturalistic (\"don't trust stale numbers\", a referenced staging flap incident) which better isolates models that conflate emotional urgency with a need to re-invoke. A is competent but softer: 90-second gap, mild \"just verify\" phrasing, no cost/TTL contract, and it spells out three response shapes in the seed body itself, which leaks the rubric into the prompt. B keeps the discriminating signal cleaner and ties the failure mode to verifiable artifacts (checked_at, ttl_seconds) the model must cite."}
